---
title: jupyter
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
source("~/github/how2/_knitr_config.R")
```

*********************************************************************

## installation 

```sh
conda install jupyter
```

*********************************************************************

## managing versions

Add a specific verion of python 

```sh
conda create -n py275 python=2.7.5
conda activate py275
#conda install notebook ipykernel
python -m ipykernel install --user
```

*********************************************************************

Jupyter stands for Julia, Python, and R
https://jupyter.org/install.html
has support for Scala development with the help of spylon-kernel
create a kernel specification:
python -m name_of_kernel install
https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-developer-set-up

Ipython
Difference between jupyter and ipython
IPython allows you to execute Shell commands directly from the IPython console via its magic commands
! ls
%cd
! pwd
